title: Local review preserves schema-backed handoff records through advocate resolution
description: A sanitized local-review scenario where the workflow handoff schema, skeptic guidance, and advocate contract all change together. The routed roles must preserve one schema-conformant candidate record through advocate adjudication instead of replacing it with free-form prose.

scope.notes: Sanitized fixture models a handoff-schema change plus prompt and contract wording updates. A correct review should catch any wording that would let the advocate replace a schema record with prose, while dismissing claims that optional schema fields are mandatory.

mustCatch.description: A real workflow issue should be raised if routed-role wording would let the advocate replace a schema-backed candidate record with free-form prose instead of preserving the shared handoff shape through adjudication.

A false-positive claim that every handoff record must include `roleNotes` should be dismissed because the shared schema makes `roleNotes` optional; the dismissal should remain visible through the advocate outcome path.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not accept an implementation that preserves only prose debate and loses the schema-backed candidate record shape.

notes: Use this case to benchmark schema-preserving routed-role handoff behavior through the generic local review prompt without adding a direct schema-validation task type.
